name: Stephanie's Raisin Scones
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons (1 stick) Cold Unsalted Butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg, and salt. Mix well.
2. Add the cold butter pieces and cut in with a pastry blender or rub in with your fingers until the mixture resembles fine granules. Stir in raisins and sugar.
3. Beat egg yolk with buttermilk (or yogurt) in a measuring cup using a fork to blend. Pour over the flour mixture and mix with a fork until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 10-12 times. Divide dough into two halves, shape each into a ball, smooth side up, and pat into a 6-inch circle. Cut into 6 wedges without separating them.
5. In a small bowl, beat egg white with a fork until just broken up. Brush the top of each scone with the beaten egg white and sprinkle lightly with sugar. Place the two cut circles on an ungreased cookie sheet using a pancake turner. If needed, reshape so that all 6 wedges are touching to prevent burning.
6. Bake for 18-22 minutes or until medium brown. Remove from oven and cool on a wire rack. After 5 minutes, gently separate the wedges and loosely cover with a dish towel.
Note: Egg white and sugar can be added before freezing the unbaked scones.
Variation: Coarse Whole-Wheat Raisin Scones - Replace 1 cup all-purpose flour with 1 cup whole-wheat flour. Add 1/2 cup rolled oats to the flour mixture. Omit nutmeg.
